sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: NavigationHeader is required as the page opener. For a Validator at the decision stage, the minimal variant focuses attention on the conversion path while maintaining wayfinding context. The persistent CTA in the nav serves as a constant upgrade prompt for trial users evaluating whether to commit.

sections.name: Hero — Value Confirmation
sections.rationale: HeroPrimary opens the page by reinforcing the core value proposition for Validator users at the decision stage. The Validator variant includes a trust_signal (e.g., 'Trusted by 10,000+ teams') above the fold, which is critical for trial users who need confidence before committing to a paid plan. The with-social-proof variant anchors credibility immediately.

sections.name: Social Proof — Credibility Anchor
sections.rationale: SocialProofBar placed directly after the hero builds immediate trust for the Validator archetype at the decision stage. The Validator variant leads with a compelling quote from a recognizable customer followed by logo clusters, providing the external validation that Validators require before converting from trial to paid.

sections.name: Outcome Metrics — Quantified Proof
sections.rationale: StatBlock delivers hard evidence for Validators at the decision stage who need measurable proof that the product delivers results. Source-attributed stats (e.g., '47% faster onboarding — based on 2,400 customer accounts') satisfy the Validator's need for verifiable claims and build the rational case for upgrading.

sections.name: Feature Value — What You Unlock
sections.rationale: FeatureGrid at the decision stage articulates what trial users gain by upgrading, framed as outcome-oriented benefit statements rather than feature names — exactly what the Validator archetype needs. The 3-col-icon layout presents a structured, scannable comparison of capabilities that reinforces the value gap between trial and paid tiers.

sections.name: Testimonial — Peer Validation
sections.rationale: TestimonialSingle is purpose-built for the Validator archetype at the decision stage. A single featured testimonial with full attribution (name, role, company, photo) placed near the pricing section serves as the final trust signal before the conversion moment. Validators weigh peer evidence heavily when making purchase decisions.

sections.name: Pricing — Plan Selection
sections.rationale: PricingCard is the primary decision-stage conversion mechanism. The Validator variant includes detailed descriptions and longer feature lists, giving trial users the thorough comparison they need to justify the purchase. The highlighted variant on the recommended plan guides the decision without pressuring — respecting the Validator's need to feel they chose rationally.

sections.name: Final Conversion — Closing CTA
sections.rationale: CTABanner closes the page narrative as a terminal conversion prompt for Validators at the decision stage. The Validator variant includes a subhead reinforcing the value proposition and a trust_signal (e.g., 'Cancel anytime — no lock-in contracts') that removes final friction for trial users hesitating to upgrade. Positioned above the footer to recapture scrollers who passed the pricing section.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: FooterMinimal is required as the page closer. The with-cta variant is essential on this decision-stage conversion page, providing a final recovery CTA for departing Validator users who may need one last prompt before leaving. Keeps legal and trust signals visible to reinforce credibility.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. At decision stage, focus must narrow to conversion action.
Validator variant always includes trust_signal. with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster, addressing validator's need for credibility confirmation.

slots.headline: Your trial proved it. Now make it permanent.
slots.subhead: Teams that upgrade from trial close design cycles 2.3x faster and ship with fewer revision loops — measured across 4,800 accounts.
slots.trust_signal: Trusted by 12,000+ product teams at companies like Notion, Loom, and Vercel

page.reasoning: Validator variant leads with quote, logos below. This combination delivers both narrative credibility and recognizable brand validation essential for trial-to-paid conversion.

slots.headline: What teams say after switching from trial
slots.quote: We cut our design-to-handoff time from 11 days to 4. MODE paid for itself in the first sprint.
slots.attribution: Priya Anand, Head of Product Design — Stackline

page.reasoning: Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust. 3-stat is the preferred default.

slots.headline: What paid customers measure after upgrading

stats.label: Faster design-to-handoff cycles compared to trial usage
Reduction in stakeholder revision requests within 60 days of upgrade
Average onboarding time for new team members added to paid workspaces

page.reasoning: Validator benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions — not feature names, benefit statements. 3-col-icon is the default for structured capability articulation.

slots.headline: What paid unlocks that your trial doesn't
slots.subhead: Six capabilities that directly reduce rework, accelerate shipping, and keep teams aligned without extra meetings.

features.title: Unlimited component libraries
features.description: Maintain a single source of truth across every product surface — no more diverging design copies between teams.

features.title: Analytics on design decisions
features.description: See which components get overridden most and where handoffs stall, so you fix process problems before they compound.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: Protect production components from accidental edits while giving stakeholders read-only visibility they actually use.

features.title: Real-time sync across workspaces
features.description: Changes propagate to every connected project in under 3 seconds — reviewed and confirmed by 98% of paid users in post-upgrade surveys.

features.title: Native developer handoff exports
features.description: Eliminate the translation layer between design and engineering with token-ready exports that match your existing code architecture.

features.title: Priority support with < 4 hr response
features.description: Get a named support contact who knows your setup — not a ticket queue. Median first response time: 3.7 hours.

page.reasoning: Always include role and company for Validator credibility — anonymous quotes carry no weight. with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity at decision moment.

slots.quote: I spent two weeks in the trial convinced it was good but not sure it was worth it. Three weeks after upgrading, I couldn't tell you what we were doing before. The analytics alone changed how we present design decisions to leadership.

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes description and longer feature list. highlighted variant for recommended plan guides validator toward conversion decision.

slots.description: Everything trial users rely on, plus the analytics, access controls, and integrations that make MODE the system your whole org works from.
features: Role-based access control for up to 50 members
Real-time sync across all connected projects
Native developer handoff with token exports
Priority support — < 4 hr median response time

page.reasoning: Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). Centered layout with reassurance messaging reduces friction at final conversion gate.

slots.headline: Your trial data doesn't disappear. Your work is already here.
slots.subhead: Every component, file, and workspace you built during the trial carries over instantly. Upgrading takes under two minutes.
slots.trust_signal: Cancel anytime — no lock-in contracts, no cancellation fees

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows. Critical recovery mechanism for validators abandoning at decision stage.
